brief_justification: Correct answer (No) with valid causal reasoning through both actual and counterfactual worlds; Multi-level analysis tracing causal chain through sister→BP→heart and identifying mutual exclusivity of conditions; Some implicit self-monitoring in structured step-by-step approach but no explicit bias checking; Basic verification through examining both worlds and confirming consistency, plus noting the mutual exclusivity as a sanity check; Acknowledges the closed-world assumption but doesn't explicitly discuss uncertainty about default BP or heart values; Identifies the key alternative (high BP without sister) and explains why it fails, but doesn't systematically consider other possible interpretations; Complete derivation trail from causal graph through both worlds to conclusion, fully reproducible
